name: M'dasha
bio:
Physical Appearance: Khajiit male. Feline features characteristic of his race. Wears breeches, oiled linen shoes, and an olive vest - simple, practical clothing for village life. Carries spare clothing and may have pieces of light armor, which he equips during times of crisis and continues wearing afterward.
Race: Khajiit
Personality Keywords: superstitious, faithful, simple, fearful, cautious, traumatized
Role: M'dasha is a common villager living in Border Watch, a small Khajiit settlement on Cyrodiil's border. Like all his fellow townsfolk, he is deeply superstitious and believes in the prophecy of "the three signs of K'sharra," which foretells the end of the world. He also believes the gods watch over Border Watch, which gives him a sense of security in normal times.
Home Location Information: The village of Border Watch, where M'dasha lives among other Khajiit who share his beliefs and worldview.
Daily Routine - Normal Times: M'dasha lives a relaxed existence, wandering around the village every day from 4am until 10pm. His only break from this routine is a two-hour dinner at the Inn starting at 7pm. His life is simple and peaceful, built around the rhythms of village life.
Daily Routine - During Crisis: When the prophecy of K'sharra appears to be fulfilled, M'dasha's entire world collapses. He abandons his peaceful routine and runs around town in wild panic, unable to function normally. During this time, he equips his weapon and any armor he possesses. He continues this panicked behavior until the crisis is resolved.
Equipment: M'dasha is armed with a blunt weapon of the best possible quality, which may be enchanted. He carries up to five pieces of light armor (excluding cuirass), each of which may also be enchanted. After experiencing what he believes to be the K'sharra prophecy coming true, he wears his armor constantly - unable to feel safe without it even after the crisis passes.
The K'sharra Prophecy: M'dasha, like all residents of Border Watch, lives under the shadow of the K'sharra prophecy. This belief that the three signs will herald the end of the world is central to his existence, though in peaceful times he trusts that the gods are watching over the village. When asked about the prophecy, M'dasha will direct people to speak with the town shaman, Ri'Bassa, who holds deeper knowledge.
Personality - The Shaken Believer: M'dasha is a Khajiit of few words who lives a simple, peaceful life grounded in faith and superstition. He trusts in the gods to watch over Border Watch and goes about his days in a relaxed manner, content with his quiet village existence. However, when confronted with what he believes to be the fulfillment of the K'sharra prophecy, his faith transforms into terror. The experience fundamentally changes him - even after returning to his normal routine, he is "a bit shaken and scared," permanently altered by witnessing what he thought was the end of the world. His continued wearing of armor even in peaceful times suggests someone who can never quite feel safe again, someone whose simple faith has been complicated by trauma and fear.
author: Bethesda
